idea-plugin.name: AI Test Case Generator
idea-plugin.description:
AI Test Case Generator automatically writes JUnit 5 unit tests for your Java methods directly inside IntelliJ IDEA, eliminating the repetitive work of authoring test boilerplate by hand.

Place the caret anywhere inside a Java method, right-click, and choose Generate Unit Test with AI. The plugin inspects the method signature, parameters, return type, annotations, field dependencies, and imports using IntelliJ's PSI engine, then sends that enriched context to the Groq AI API. Within seconds, a complete test class is written to the correct src/test/java/ location — matching your package structure automatically. If the file cannot be placed on disk, the generated code falls back to the clipboard so you never lose the output.

What each generated test class includes:
  • Happy-path tests — valid inputs that exercise the main logic branch
  • Edge-case tests — null inputs, empty collections, boundary values, and zero
  • Error-case tests — invalid arguments, expected exceptions, and failure paths
  • Mockito setup — dependencies are stubbed with @Mock and @InjectMocks
  • Readable test names — every method carries a @DisplayName that reads like a sentence
  • Arrange-Act-Assert structure — each test body follows the standard AAA pattern
Requirements:
  • IntelliJ IDEA 2024.1 or later (Community or Ultimate)
  • Java project with JUnit 5 and Mockito on the test classpath
  • A Groq API key (free tier available at console.groq.com)
Setup (one time):
  1. Open Settings / Preferences → Tools → AI Test Generator
  2. Paste your Groq API key
  3. Optionally select a different Groq model or adjust the max-token limit
Your API key is stored using IntelliJ's built-in PasswordSafe credential store and is never written to disk in plain text or included in project files.

Privacy notice: When you invoke the action, the plugin sends the following data from your project to the Groq API (api.groq.com), a third-party service operated by Groq, Inc.: the method body, class and package name, field declarations, imports, annotations, and related type signatures of the method under test. No other files or project data are transmitted. Please review Groq's privacy policy before use.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.2 — Stability and safety improvements
  • Confirmation dialog shown before overwriting an existing test file
  • Improved performance — HTTP connection pool is now reused across calls
0.1.1 — Compatibility update
  • Verified and confirmed compatible with IntelliJ IDEA 2024.1 through 2026.1
  • Resolved JetBrains Marketplace compatibility verifier warnings
0.1.0 — Initial release
  • Generate JUnit 5 tests from any Java method via right-click or Alt+Insert
  • PSI-based context extraction — reads imports, fields, annotations, superclass, and related types
  • Groq API integration with configurable model and token limits
  • Automatic test file placement at the conventional src/test/java/ path
  • Clipboard fallback when the test file cannot be created on disk
  • Secure API key storage via the IDE PasswordSafe credential store

action.description: Generate JUnit 5 unit tests for the method at the caret using Groq AI
